skill.name: caveman-stats
skill.description: Shows real Claude Code session token usage and estimated savings from the local session log. It is triggered by /caveman-stats and reports numbers provided by the mode-tracker hook.
skill.summary: Show actual session token usage and estimated savings without model-side estimation.
skill.author: juliusbrussee

security_audit.summary: Static external command findings are false positives from Markdown backticks and fenced examples, not executable code. The path traversal and weak cryptography findings are also false positives, but the skill declares local session-log reading and file writing through a hook, which creates a legitimate filesystem privacy concern.

medium_findings.title: Declared Session Log Filesystem Access
medium_findings.description: The skill states that a hook reads the current Claude Code session log and writes a lifetime-savings suffix file. This is expected for the feature, but local session logs can contain sensitive conversation data or metadata if implementation boundaries are too broad.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The behavior is explicitly described in both audited files. No hook source was present in the scanned skill files, so implementation safety cannot be fully verified here.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Markdown Backtick Execution Findings
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick execution patterns are Markdown inline code, a slash-command example, and formatted sample output. No executable shell or Ruby backtick operation appears in the audited files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines are prose, Markdown fences, inline code references, or example output. They are not executable script content.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Documentation Link Path Traversal Finding
low_findings.description: The path traversal pattern is a relative Markdown link to the repository README. It does not perform filesystem access or resolve user-controlled paths.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is a Markdown reference link only. There is no code that opens or traverses paths from that link.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Weak Cryptography Finding
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptographic algorithm location is YAML front matter text. No weak cryptographic algorithm or cryptographic operation is present at that location.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 starts the description field in front matter. It does not mention or invoke MD5, SHA1, DES, or another weak cryptographic primitive.

dangerous_patterns.title: Local Session Log Reading
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill depends on a hook that reads the current Claude Code session log from disk. This pattern needs strict scoping because session logs may contain sensitive user prompts and responses.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The documentation directly states that session logs are read. The scanned files do not include the hook implementation, so the exact read scope is not verifiable.

content.value_statement: Teams need accurate token usage data instead of rough estimates. This skill reports session tokens and estimated savings from Claude Code session logs.
actual_capabilities: Reports actual input and output token counts for the current session.
Shows estimated savings against a non-caveman baseline.
Uses the local Claude Code session log instead of model estimates.
Displays a formatted stats response when /caveman-stats is invoked.
Supports a statusline suffix for lifetime savings totals.
limitations: Requires the related mode-tracker hook to be installed and active.
Only reports data available in the local Claude Code session log.
Savings are estimates, even when token counts are read from logs.
The scanned skill files do not include the hook implementation.

use_cases.title: Monitor Daily Token Use
use_cases.description: Check current session token totals while working in Claude Code.

use_cases.title: Review Prompt Efficiency
use_cases.description: Compare actual output tokens with estimated savings after using concise interaction modes.

use_cases.title: Track Team Usage Habits
use_cases.description: Use session reports to discuss usage patterns and reduce unnecessary token spend.

prompt_templates.title: Show Current Stats
prompt_templates.prompt: Run /caveman-stats for my current Claude Code session.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner check of current token usage.

prompt_templates.title: Explain The Report
prompt_templates.prompt: Run /caveman-stats and explain what each number means in plain language.
prompt_templates.scenario: Basic interpretation of the stats output.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Session Efficiency
prompt_templates.prompt: Run /caveman-stats and help me identify whether this session used tokens efficiently.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare Usage Summary
prompt_templates.prompt: Run /caveman-stats and summarize the savings signal for a team usage review.

output_examples.input: User asks for current session stats.
output_examples.output: The response shows session turn count, input tokens, output tokens, baseline estimate, and saved-token percentage.

output_examples.input: User checks whether the concise mode saved tokens.
output_examples.output: The response highlights estimated savings and separates measured token counts from baseline estimates.

output_examples.input: User wants a team-friendly usage note.
output_examples.output: The response gives a short summary of token usage, savings direction, and what the numbers can support.

best_practices: Use the report after meaningful work sessions, not after every small prompt.
Treat savings as directional estimates, not billing records.
Review local hook permissions before enabling session-log access.
anti_patterns: Do not use the report as the only source for official billing reconciliation.
Do not enable the hook where session logs contain data that must not be read.
Do not compare sessions without considering task size and complexity.

faq.question: Does this skill estimate token usage with the model?
faq.answer: No. It states that token counts come from the local Claude Code session log.

faq.question: Does it need filesystem access?
faq.answer: Yes. The documented behavior reads a local session log and writes a status suffix file.

faq.question: Can it show exact cost savings?
faq.answer: No. It shows actual token counts and estimated savings against a baseline.

faq.question: Is the hook implementation included in the scanned files?
faq.answer: No. The audited files describe hooks, but the hook source was not included in this skill folder.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported tools.
